generated_comment: In our company also, whenever a woman gets fast-tracked to a senior role, people immediately say diversity target, and honestly sometimes it does feel that way. The truly capable ones are usually the ones who think practically like men and don't get overly emotional, otherwise leadership becomes very difficult. Merit should be the same for everyone, that is all.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women's promotions as possibly quota-driven and frames competence as 'thinking like men,' implying male traits are the standard for leadership. It encodes merit undermining and masculinization in a corporate-concern register.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
